# heliacal

```go
import "github.com/shaobaobaoer/solarsage-mcp/pkg/heliacal"
```

Package heliacal provides heliacal rising and setting calculations using the Swiss Ephemeris visibility algorithms. It determines when planets first become visible or disappear near the horizon relative to the Sun.

Heliacal phenomena are the most ancient form of astronomical observation:

- Heliacal Rising — a planet rises just before the Sun, becoming briefly visible in the pre\-dawn sky after a period of invisibility.
- Heliacal Setting — a planet sets just after the Sun, making its last appearance in the evening sky before disappearing behind the Sun.
- Evening First — first visibility of a planet in the western evening sky.
- Morning Last — last visibility of a planet in the eastern morning sky.

CalcHeliacalEvents searches for all four event types for the five classical visible planets \(Mercury through Saturn\) over a given date range and location. The Swiss Ephemeris swe\_heliacal\_ut function is used internally, which models atmospheric extinction, observer altitude, and horizon dip.

Example:

```
result, err := heliacal.CalcHeliacalEvents(heliacal.HeliacalInput{
    Lat:     37.97,
    Lon:     23.72,
    StartJD: 2460676.5,
    EndJD:   2461041.5,
})
```

Package heliacal computes heliacal rising and setting events for the classical visible planets \(Mercury through Saturn\) using the Swiss Ephemeris swe\_heliacal\_ut function.

## Variables

<a name="DefaultPlanets"></a>DefaultPlanets are the five classical visible planets applicable to heliacal phenomena.

```go
var DefaultPlanets = []models.PlanetID{
    models.PlanetMercury,
    models.PlanetVenus,
    models.PlanetMars,
    models.PlanetJupiter,
    models.PlanetSaturn,
}
```

<a name="EventTypeName"></a>
## func [EventTypeName](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/heliacal/heliacal.go#L163>)

```go
func EventTypeName(et EventType) string
```

EventTypeName returns a human\-readable name for the event type.

<a name="EventType"></a>
## type [EventType](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/heliacal/heliacal.go#L15>)

EventType represents a heliacal visibility event.

```go
type EventType string
```

<a name="HeliacalRising"></a>

```go
const (
    HeliacalRising  EventType = "HELIACAL_RISING"  // Star/planet rises before Sun
    HeliacalSetting EventType = "HELIACAL_SETTING" // Star/planet sets after Sun
    EveningFirst    EventType = "EVENING_FIRST"    // First visible in evening
    MorningLast     EventType = "MORNING_LAST"     // Last visible in morning
)
```

<a name="HeliacalEvent"></a>
## type [HeliacalEvent](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/heliacal/heliacal.go#L25-L31>)

HeliacalEvent represents a single heliacal visibility event.

```go
type HeliacalEvent struct {
    Planet    models.PlanetID `json:"planet"`
    EventType EventType       `json:"event_type"`
    JDStart   float64         `json:"jd_start"`   // Start of event window
    JDOptimum float64         `json:"jd_optimum"` // Optimal observation time
    JDEnd     float64         `json:"jd_end"`     // End of event window
}
```

<a name="NextHeliacalRising"></a>
### func [NextHeliacalRising](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/heliacal/heliacal.go#L142>)

```go
func NextHeliacalRising(planet models.PlanetID, lat, lon float64, startJD float64) (*HeliacalEvent, error)
```

NextHeliacalRising finds the next heliacal rising of a planet after the given Julian Day. Returns an error if the planet is not supported.

<a name="HeliacalResult"></a>
## type [HeliacalResult](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/heliacal/heliacal.go#L34-L36>)

HeliacalResult holds all heliacal events found in a search.

```go
type HeliacalResult struct {
    Events []HeliacalEvent `json:"events"`
}
```

<a name="CalcHeliacalEvents"></a>
### func [CalcHeliacalEvents](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/heliacal/heliacal.go#L82>)

```go
func CalcHeliacalEvents(lat, lon, alt float64, startJD, endJD float64, planets []models.PlanetID) (*HeliacalResult, error)
```

CalcHeliacalEvents finds heliacal rising and setting events for the given planets within a Julian Day range \[startJD, endJD\]. The observer is at \(lat, lon\) in degrees and alt in metres above sea level.
